info.name: DiskMap
info.summary: See where your storage is going — treemap and top consumers, instance-wide
info.description:
DiskMap shows where storage is going across your Nextcloud instance — per user, per team folder and per group — without scanning anything.
Nextcloud already tracks aggregated folder sizes in its file cache, updated on every operation. DiskMap is a visualizer over data that already exists: it never triggers a filesystem scan.
**For administrators:** - Team folders overview: used space, quota and occupancy, with a files / trash / versions breakdown for each folder. - Top largest files across the instance or within any scope. - Group view with used-space attribution, reconciled against overlaps (a folder shared by several groups is shown as such, not silently divided).
**For every user:** - The same treemap and top-consumers view, scoped strictly to files you can access.
All figures show the timestamp of the underlying file cache entry, so it's always clear this reflects what Nextcloud's cache knows, not a live disk read.
info.author: Ricardo Ferreira
